name: Ramla
title: Mother of Qasim ibn Hasan
shortDescription: The wife of Imam Hasan (AS) and mother of Qasim, who witnessed her thirteen-year-old son's body being torn apart in Karbala.
fullStory:
Ramla (or Umm Farwa) was the loyal wife of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS) who lived under the protection of her brother-in-law Imam Hussain (AS) after her husband's martyrdom. She was the mother of Qasim, Abu Bakr, and Abdullah ibn Hasan.

Ramla was present in the Karbala caravan with her children. She was the mother who prepared her teenage son (Qasim) for the battlefield with her own hands. When Qasim received permission to fight from his uncle, Ramla sent him off with tearful eyes but a firm heart.

The peak of Ramla's tragedy was when she heard Qasim's cry for help. She witnessed how the fragile body of her thirteen-year-old son was trampled under the hooves of the enemy's horses, and how his uncle Hussain (AS) brought his torn body back to the tents.

After Ashura, this mourning mother, who had lost three sons in Karbala, was taken to Kufa and Sham with bound hands along with Zaynab (AS) and other captives, becoming the messenger of her sons' blood.
quoteArabic: يا بُنَيَّ! أَمّا أَنْتَ فَقَدْ قَضَيْتَ ما عَلَيْكَ...
quote: (Condition in Qasim's mourning:) O my dear son! You have fulfilled your duty and sacrificed your life for your uncle...
